ab: kiya pama yau imi ta pateRungan qemizuan, temaqsi nani, matalin ti sa sulin, yau ti ta tukayan qemizuan.
ch: 小時候我們家住在新社，上學以後，就搬到了樹林，一直在都市裡居住。
en: When we were young, our family lived in pateRungan. After I started school, we moved to Shulin and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: temaqsi ti ta Rayan taqsian nani, sinanam iku tu ussiq sataqsian tu zana yencumin ay, mesuppaR ti ta lawlawan ay yencumin yau naququni ay muwaza wawaway na.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Raqana, hibangan na tasaw setangi, aiku seRay Runanay ay qaqa temanan sa damu melaut tu baqian, ngid qasuppaR tu naquni ya wawaway na zata.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: uwalu betin ti tasaw na baqian, wanayka taytan ta zau sunis pama.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: pasanu iku mesuppaR tu utani sikawman, nengi tenankebalan na, suppaR temanbusus qaya, tamankizaya seRay temanzipun.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的噶瑪蘭語說得很流利，也會說閩語、阿美語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in kebalan, as well as Hokkien, Pangcah and Japanese.

ab: pasanu iku timazipana qawka mesuppaR, timangu kiya pama sinanam ti tu sikawman na zipun, kizay ya lazat ya napawan na, ta qakelawkawayan na mazemun qenanaman na tu lazat na busus, Raqana mesuppaR aizipna tu muwaza sikawman.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是阿美族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Pangcah, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: ta nipasanuan niaq nani, semanu ya baqian tu muwaza qenasuppaR na waway na semalaw masang.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: yau muwaza qenaRayngan niaq mipil, wanayka paninguan niaq tu azazasaz ay simaipilan, qanengi muman paqangi mipil.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: kiya pama mai iku mesuppaR tu naquni waway na kebalan, yencumin ya aizipna yau qanasikazan na.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: muwaza ti qenasuppaRan nani, meRakebalan ay lazat ta aneman na, maken tatezuma, qaRuzangian ay qaya.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為噶瑪蘭族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a kebalan is unique and something to be proud of.
